answer:
**Parāśara Smṛti** (Chapter 1) provides the most detailed prescriptive rules for *atithi*-hospitality (a guest arriving unexpectedly during the *Vaiśvadeva* ritual, typically a weary traveler from afar, excluding locals or prior arrivals). It lists the six daily duties including guest-worship, defines a qualifying *atithi*, mandates honoring with *arghya*-water, seat, foot-washing, food, and escort; neglecting this prevents ancestors from accepting offerings for 15 years and renders fire-oblations futile.[^1][^2]

**Manusmṛti** (Chapter 4, covering laws of the householder including the five great sacrifices) similarly defines an *atithi* as a Brahmin staying one night (excluding locals or social visitors), requiring a seat, water, food per ability with respect; a guest sent by the sun at sunset must not be turned away unfed.[^3]

**Yājñavalkya Smṛti** (Chapter 1) frames guest-hospitality as one of the five *mahāyajñas* (great sacrifices to men), to be performed daily alongside others; a traveler or Vedic *śrotriya* merits special honor as a bridge to Brahman's world.[^4]

Less prescriptive mentions appear in **Aṅgirasa Smṛti** (Chapter 1, guest-worship before other rites, feeding gurus/elders/guests)[^5] and **Brhaspati Smṛti** (Chapter 1, honoring aged/guests with offerings),[^6] but no comparable rules. Ethnographic discussions of hospitality norms occur in Charles Comte's *Traité de Législation* (Vols. II-IV), but lack formal "rules." No other works cover prescriptive rules for guest-hospitality.

[^1]: Parāśara Smṛti, [Chapter 1](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/01/translation/#p-40daa2).
[^2]: Parāśara Smṛti, [Chapter 1](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/01/translation/#p-abc05c), [Chapter 1](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/01/translation/#p-20eb98), [Chapter 1](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/01/translation/#p-36fe04), [Chapter 1](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/01/translation/#p-189326), [Chapter 1](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/01/translation/#p-d461ee), [Chapter 1](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/01/translation/#p-51e1c4).
[^3]: Manusmṛti, [Chapter 4](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/04/translation/#p-de0b29), [Chapter 4](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/04/translation/#p-499a59), [Chapter 4](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/04/translation/#p-f3cdd6).
[^4]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, [Chapter 1](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/01/translation/#p-76e5e9).
[^5]: Āṅgirasa Smṛti, [Chapter 1](/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-b4c044).
[^6]: Bṛhaspati Smṛti, [Chapter 1](/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-a9c1cd).
